Empires & Puzzles Louhi 5 Star Legendary Dark Purple from Kalevala Family Review
Louhi was once the queen of the north, stripping buffs and lowering defenses. Does this original Kalevala witch still have the magic to compete, or has her costume stolen all the glory?
Louhi: The Witch of the North Who Dispels Your Hopes (and Buffs)
Louhi: The Original Wicked Witch of the North – Still Casting Spells?
, is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the

event. Released back in the summer of 2022, she was designed as a utility powerhouse, combining a dispel-first mechanic with defense reduction. While her costumed "Mistress" variant has recently taken the spotlight with modern stats, this review focuses on the original base form. She represents an era where utility was king, but in today's high-stat meta, she faces an uphill battle to stay relevant without her costume's wardrobe upgrade.

Louhi's design is delightfully hag-like, a true representation of the formidable witch from Finnish mythology. She isn't a "waifu" or a shining knight; she's a terrifying, bird-like crone with wings and a scowl that could curdle milk. The dark, muted purple tones fit her role perfectly. It's a refreshing break from the usual glamorous heroes—sometimes you just want to play as the villain who looks the part.

When we look at Louhi's base stats (Power: 937, Attack: 927, Defense: 865, Health: 1759), the age gaps start to show. Compared to modern juggernauts releasing in late 2025 and 2026 (who frequently push 1260+ Power and 1300+ stats across the board), Louhi falls into the "dated" category. While a balance update gave her a nudge, she is significantly squishier and hits softer than the current standard. She relies entirely on her
class to withstand status ailments and her passive to keep her alive, as her raw durability is no longer impressive.
is a textbook utility skill. The order of operations is critical here:

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

bonus reduces damage received from the character's stronger element (Holy in this case). This makes her surprisingly tanky against Yellow titans or mono-Yellow teams, stacking nicely with her innate resistance to Holy ailments.

is excellent. Gaining mana and health when critically injured can disrupt the enemy's timing. If an enemy sniper leaves her with 100 HP, she suddenly heals back up and gains a chunk of mana, potentially allowing her to fire her dispel and turn the tide.

is a basic Aether Power. While extra damage is never bad, for a utility hero like Louhi, something defensive (like Damage Reduction) or supportive (like Ailment Immunity) might have been more impactful to ensure she survives to cast her dispel.
Upon release, Louhi was a top-tier solution to the "buff heavy" meta. A purple dispel-all hero who also dropped defense was rare and highly improved purple stacks against titans and in wars.

The game has moved on. 250% damage is now 4-star territory, and -24% defense down is weak compared to costumes like Tiburtus or newer 5-stars. Her stats leave her vulnerable to being one-shot by modern snipers.

Without her costume, base Louhi is hard to justify in top-tier play. She is still useful for newer players who need a 5-star dispeller, or in specific Rush Attack wars where her speed is mitigated, but for veteran rosters, she has been retired to the "Soul Exchange" fodder list unless you pull her costume.
This is where the magic happens—literally.
The costume boosts her Power to ~1220 and stats to modern levels (1185 Atk / 1235 Def / 2390 HP).

If you are using Base Louhi, it's likely because you need her utility to survive.

Louhi is best used as a "can opener"—she goes first to strip defenses and buffs, allowing your real hitters to do the work.

Being Average speed, she is vulnerable to Fast mana controllers like

If you pull Louhi early, she is a fantastic asset. A 5-star dispel-all hero is a vital tool for map stages and raids. Level her up, but maybe hold off on Limit Breaking her until you see if you can get her costume or a more modern hero.

Without the costume, she is likely destined for the Soul Exchange. Her base stats just can't keep up with 2026 heroes. If you use her, it's strictly for her dispel in War depth, but don't expect her to survive heavy fire.
"The dispel first is the main upside to this hero. The damage and then def down are also good, but the dispel makes it great."
- A player noting her utility upon release.
"This chick does virtually no damage at 250% on Average with little to nothing else going on... extremely underwhelming."
- A critical take on her damage output.

Louhi was originally seen as a solid, if not overpowered, utility hero. The community correctly identified that "Priority Dispel" is an evergreen skill, but predicted her damage would age poorly—a prediction that has come true.

Base Louhi is a reminder of a simpler time in Empires & Puzzles. Her kit is mechanically sound—Priority Dispel is always useful—but her numbers have been left in the dust. She's like an old reliable Nokia phone in a world of smartphones: she does the basic job (dispelling) perfectly, but don't expect her to run any high-performance apps (damage/survival). If you have the costume, she's a goddess. If not, she's a niche utility pick at best.
A detailed review of Louhi, the 5-star Dark hero from the Kalevala family. We analyze her stats, special skill, and viability in the modern meta compared to her powerful costume.
